title: The Well-Stocked Pantry
subtitle: Useful staples to keep around so EWHF cooking is easier on an ordinary day.

items.title: Stocks and Broths
body: Keep a few flavor-building liquids available. They make soups, stews, grains, sauces, beans, and pan reductions taste fuller without needing cream or heavy finishes.

items.title: Beans, Lentils, and Legumes
body: Beans and lentils are some of the most useful EWHF staples. They bring fiber, body, protein, and substance while keeping recipes satisfying and low in cholesterol.
• Dried cannellini or Great Northern beans
• A few cans of beans for backup

items.title: Grains, Rice, and Pasta
body: Grains, rice, and pasta turn vegetables, beans, sauces, and leftovers into actual meals. Keep a useful range, not every grain on earth.

items.title: Tomatoes and Vegetables
body: Shelf-stable and frozen vegetables make it easier to cook when fresh produce is low. Tomatoes, peppers, greens, mushrooms, peas, and corn can all turn a basic dish into dinner.

items.title: Acid and Brightness
body: Acid keeps low-cholesterol food from tasting heavy or flat. Use it carefully at the end to brighten beans, soups, grains, vegetables, and sauces.

items.title: Savory Flavor Builders
body: These ingredients add depth, salt, savoriness, richness, or sharpness in small amounts. They are useful when a dish needs more than salt but not more heaviness.
• Chile crisp or chile oil, used carefully
• Worcestershire sauce, when the recipe allows it

items.title: Spices and Dry Seasonings
body: A practical spice shelf gives the cook options without becoming clutter. These are the everyday seasonings that support many EWHF recipes.

items.title: Texture Helpers
body: Texture helpers make soups, stews, beans, vegetables, and bowls feel finished. They add crunch, chew, or contrast so the dish does not eat soft all the way through.
• Crispy shallots or onions, used as a controlled garnish

items.title: Freezer Staples
body: The freezer is part of the pantry. It lets future meals borrow time from past cooking, which is especially useful when recipes depend on stock, beans, sauces, or cooked grains.

items.title: Fresh Staples Worth Replacing Often
body: A few fresh ingredients are worth keeping in regular rotation. They make pantry cooking taste alive instead of stored.
